mission.title: Install and Configure Knative Eventing (Knative) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Eventing is an event-driven application platform for Kubernetes that enables secure event processing and discovery using CloudEvents. You would install it to build applications that respond to events in a scalable and efficient manner.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Knative Eventing Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add knative-eventing https://ahhhh-man.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Knative Eventing
steps.description:
Install the Knative Eventing Helm chart into a new namespace called 'knative-eventing'.
```bash
helm install knative-eventing knative-eventing/knative-eventing --namespace knative-eventing --create-namespace --version 0.18.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Knative Eventing pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-eventing
```

steps.title: Configure default channel
steps.description:
Apply the default channel configuration for Knative Eventing.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/knative/eventing/main/config/400-config-br-default-channel.yaml --namespace knative-eventing
```

steps.title: Configure broker defaults
steps.description:
Apply the broker defaults configuration for Knative Eventing.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/knative/eventing/main/config/400-config-br-defaults.yaml --namespace knative-eventing
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Knative Eventing release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall knative-eventing --namespace knative-eventing
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by Knative Eventing.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep 'eventing.knative.dev' | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'knative-eventing' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace knative-eventing
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of Knative Eventing.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace knative-eventing -o yaml > knative-eventing-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Knative Eventing
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Knative Eventing installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade knative-eventing knative-eventing/knative-eventing --namespace knative-eventing --version 0.18.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Knative Eventing pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Knative Eventing pods are stuck in 'Pending', check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace knative-eventing
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff', check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration issues
troubleshooting.description:
If the eventing functionality is not working as expected, verify the applied configurations.
```bash
kubectl get configmap --namespace knative-eventing
kubectl describe configmap <configmap-name> --namespace knative-eventing
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Knative Eventing will have all pods running in the 'knative-eventing' namespace without errors. You can verify this by running 'kubectl get pods --namespace knative-eventing'.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add knative-eventing https://ahhhh-man.github.io/charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
